response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements of this role. His professional C++ exposure is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 (plus a bachelor thesis project), far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His career centers on DLang, TypeScript/Node.js, and web/GIS platforms. He lists SQL/MySQL/MongoDB but no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and he has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not investment banking. Desirable extras like CI/CD, Java, and scripting do not compensate for the missing mandatory pillars.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions
Broad polyglot background with some C/C++ IDE tooling work at National Instruments
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and it dates back to 2014-2015
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience (mandatory 5-10 years on Windows)
No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD practices
